name: resume_delegation
description:
Resume a prior delegation with its full conversation state loaded, then run a new sub-task on top of it.

Use this when a follow-up question targets the same area as a prior delegation. The previous delegate already knows the relevant file:line context — resuming is structurally cheaper than a cold re-delegation because the child does not have to re-discover anything.

Semantics:
- Loads the original delegation's child conversation snapshot from engine storage.
- Spawns a fresh child loop seeded with that conversation history plus your new `task` as the user message.
- Runs to completion and returns a DelegationResult to the lead exactly like entropic.delegate.
- Fails with a typed error if `delegation_id` does not exist in storage.

Use entropic.followup FIRST to find candidate delegation_ids. Use entropic.delegate (not this tool) when there is no relevant prior delegation.

Introduced in v2.1.6 (gh#32).

delegation_id.description: Storage delegation id returned by entropic.followup

task.description: New sub-task to run on top of the loaded conversation

max_turns.description: Maximum number of turns the resumed delegate can use (optional)
